subject: Add get and delete of non-default policies
commit_message:
Add get and delete of non-default policies

- Create new methods in tosca provider for get and delete of non-default policies

Issue-ID: POLICY-3256
Signed-off-by: arkadiusz.adamski <aadamski@est.tech>
Change-Id: Ia1d0e2a9c41cbedc76db4ec0c930162455a5f29a

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-models-master-verify-java/1510/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/policy-models-master-verify-java/1510

Patch Set 1:

Hmm.  I'm questioning the need for POLICY-3256, when deleteByConceptKey() can already be used to delete particular templates by name & version - seems safer than deleting by name only.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> Hmm.  I'm questioning the need for POLICY-3256, when deleteByConceptKey() can already be used to delete particular templates by name & version - seems safer than deleting by name only.

Yes name and version will only delete one record at a time which is safer.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> Hmm.  I'm questioning the need for POLICY-3256, when deleteByConceptKey() can already be used to delete particular templates by name & version - seems safer than deleting by name only.

Yes, we need to think about this more. The deelteByConceptkey() is on Dao level but is not exposed above that. Also what worries me is referential integrity, of a concept is referred to by another concept then should we allow it to be deleted? Also though, maybe we need a "super user" ability so that a "super user" can update and delete concepts in the unlikely (;-D) event of things getting mucked up.

Also I'd like to have a more generic way of doing CRUD for TOSCA concepts, we have a good few TOSCA concepts int here now that are all almost identical (type and corresponding instance), if we have a bespoke API that covers all the concepts it's going to get very unwieldy

Patch Set 1:

> Also though, maybe we need a "super user" ability so that a "super user" can update and delete concepts in the unlikely (;-D) event of things getting mucked up.

Is it time to bump the priority on user roles?

Abandoned

We need to rethink how we are working with service templates.
